title: Align installer contract across protocol, questions, registry, placeholders, and steps
description: Reconcile the actual installer contract before or alongside schema rollout. Align state keys, service and subdomain identifiers, placeholder mappings, and secret coverage across AGENT_PROTOCOL.md, questions/01-06.md, registry.yaml, lib/placeholders.md, and steps/*.md so the new schema reflects one consistent source of truth instead of preserving existing drift.
acceptance_criteria: foundation_services, selected_services, host_addons, subdomain keys, and canonical service slugs are consistently named and documented across the protocol and questions; all placeholder mappings used by templates and steps are documented in the protocol or supporting docs; secret requirements in questions/05-secrets.md, registry.yaml, and steps/60-services.md are reconciled; and any intentional differences are documented explicitly rather than implied.

title: Restore OpenCode agent fallback
description: Restore rakkib init OpenCode install offer, avoid reinstalling when already present, and default root-with-SUDO_USER bootstrap path to the sudo user's home.
close_reason: Restored OpenCode install offer, reuses existing OpenCode paths, and defaults root sudo-user bootstrap checkouts to the sudo user's home.

title: Simplify installer phases 1 2 4
description: Implement documentation collapse, step/lib trim, and shell consolidation from simplify.md, then update simplify.md with completion status.
close_reason: Implemented simplification phases 1, 2, and 4; updated simplify.md with status and verification notes.

title: Detect existing OpenCode outside PATH during init
close_reason: Fixed OpenCode detection for existing user-scoped installs

title: Make bootstrap prompt for rakkib init
description: Change install.sh so curl bootstrap only clones/updates the repo, installs the rakkib shim, and prints the rakkib init command instead of launching init automatically.
close_reason: install.sh now installs the CLI and prints rakkib init instead of launching init automatically.

title: Add sudo preauthorization keepalive
description: Improve Rakkib CLI UX by prompting for sudo before launching the agent and keeping the sudo timestamp alive while the agent session runs, without storing passwords.
close_reason: Added pre-agent sudo authorization and session-scoped sudo keepalive supervision.

title: Implement Rakkib CLI privilege split
description: Add a first-class rakkib CLI that runs orchestration as the normal user and isolates root-only actions behind explicit privileged subcommands, while preserving install.sh bootstrap compatibility.
close_reason: Implemented first-class rakkib CLI with user-first bootstrap handoff and allowlisted privileged helper actions.

title: Verify question schema coverage and consistency
description: Review all installer phases and supporting docs after the schema change to confirm there are no remaining mismatches in question style, accepted answer formats, recorded keys, placeholder mappings, or secret expectations.
acceptance_criteria: A verification pass confirms every question phase uses the schema consistently and no contradictions remain across AGENT_PROTOCOL.md, questions/01-06.md, registry.yaml, lib/placeholders.md, and the installer steps for agent-driven interviews.

title: Add schema metadata to question phases 01-06
description: Add structured metadata to each installer question file while preserving the existing human-readable guidance. Ensure every phase has machine-readable definitions for prompts, validations, defaults, and recorded state keys.
acceptance_criteria: questions/01-platform.md through questions/06-confirm.md each include schema metadata aligned with their existing flow and state writes.

title: Normalize service selection inputs
description: Refine the services phase so the checklist remains readable but canonical input uses stable service slugs instead of position numbers. Numeric entries may remain as optional aliases for convenience.
acceptance_criteria: The services question documents slug-first answers, preserves checklist rendering, and defines any numeric aliases explicitly in structured metadata.

title: Define minimal question schema
description: Define the smallest structured metadata shape needed for installer questions. Cover question types, canonical answer formats, defaults, validation, derived values, dependencies, and alias handling without introducing a heavy DSL.
acceptance_criteria: A documented schema exists for confirm, text, single_select, multi_select, and derived fields; canonical inputs are defined; optional numeric aliases are addressed; the format is small enough to layer onto existing markdown files.

title: Update AGENT_PROTOCOL for schema-backed questioning
description: Update the normative protocol so agents follow one rendering and answer-handling contract across all phases. Standardize how prompts, defaults, validations, and derived values are presented to users.
acceptance_criteria: AGENT_PROTOCOL.md defines the question interaction contract, references the structured schema, and makes canonical answer formats explicit across phases.

title: AgentSchema
description: Introduce a minimal structured installer question schema so agents present and validate questions consistently, while also unifying the installer contract across AGENT_PROTOCOL.md, question files, registry.yaml, placeholder mappings, and execution steps. Keep the existing markdown question files, add machine-readable metadata, standardize canonical reply formats across phases, and eliminate cross-file drift in recorded keys, service/subdomain identifiers, and secret coverage.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal machine-readable schema exists for installer questions; AGENT_PROTOCOL.md defines the authoritative interaction contract; question files preserve human-readable guidance while adding metadata; canonical answer formats and aliases are explicit; state keys, subdomain mappings, service identifiers, and secret coverage are aligned across protocol, questions, registry, placeholders, and steps; and a final verification pass confirms no contract drift remains for agent-driven interviews.

title: Prompt before root bootstrap
description: Replace the root bootstrap hard error with a minimal confirmation prompt and align protocol wording.
close_reason: Replaced root bootstrap hard stop with minimal confirmation prompt.

title: Add transfer.sh optional service
description: Add dutchcoders/transfer.sh as an optional unauthenticated public file-transfer service in Rakkib, including checklist warning, Docker/Caddy templates, validation, docs, and landing page updates.
close_reason: Implemented transfer.sh optional service with no-auth warning, Docker/Caddy templates, docs, validation, and landing page updates.

title: Add Hermes optional service
description: Add NousResearch Hermes Agent as an optional Rakkib host service with a dashboard exposed at hermes.<domain> behind Authentik when selected.
close_reason: Implemented Hermes optional host service with Authentik-protected dashboard route, installer docs, templates, and web UI updates.

title: Integrate VErgo terminal host addon
description: Add terminal-only VErgo integration as a Rakkib host addon, excluding macOS WM/Desktop components.
close_reason: Implemented terminal-only VErgo host addon integration

title: Add rakkib uninstall command
description: The rakkib CLI command list should include uninstall. Implement a safe user-scoped uninstall that removes the ~/.local/bin/rakkib shim and the managed ~/.bashrc PATH block without deleting the repo or deployed services.
close_reason: Added safe rakkib uninstall command and verified help, syntax, and isolated HOME cleanup

title: Add bash PATH setup for rakkib shim
description: install.sh installs the rakkib shim into ~/.local/bin, but users may not have that directory on PATH. Add an idempotent ~/.bashrc update and clear next-step messaging so users can source ~/.bashrc and run rakkib.
close_reason: Implemented idempotent bash PATH setup in install.sh and verified shell syntax

title: revisit the root installation with sudo
add catogerizition to services in frontend
Document schema authoring and migration
description: Document how future contributors should add or change installer questions using the new schema. Include examples and guardrails to prevent drift between prose and metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Contributor-facing docs explain the schema, show one or more examples, and describe how to keep prose guidance and metadata aligned.
